scenarios.title: last paragraph of a long page scrolls into view and is in the viewport @positive
scenarios.userAction: Navigate to a long HTML demo page, scroll the last paragraph element into view using scrollIntoViewIfNeeded(), and assert it is visible in the viewport.
expectedAssertions: last paragraph (p:last-child) is in the viewport after scrollIntoViewIfNeeded() — verified via toBeInViewport()

locatorTable.notes: External public demo page — data-testid cannot be added. CSS structural pseudo-selector is direct mechanical mapping. page.locator('p').last() is an alternative but semantically different (last-child of parent vs. last paragraph in document order). See pin 1 and Q1.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q1 unresolved: p:last-child preserved from source; DOM of external page not inspected — may differ from page.locator('p').last().
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Open https://bonigarcia.dev/selenium-webdriver-java/long-page.html in DevTools, run document.querySelector('p:last-child') and document.querySelectorAll('p')[document.querySelectorAll('p').length-1] — if different, use page.locator('p').last() instead.
